hawkish_phrases: Almost all participants noted that in their economic projections that they judged that additional increases in the target federal funds rate during 2023 would be appropriate
upside risks to the inflation outlook or the possibility that persistently high inflation might cause inflation expectations to become unanchored remained key factors shaping the policy outlook
Some participants favored raising the target range for the federal funds rate 25 basis points at this meeting
maintaining a restrictive stance for monetary policy would be appropriate
dovish_phrases: Holding the target range steady at this meeting allows the Committee to assess additional information
Most participants observed that uncertainty about the outlook for the economy and inflation remained elevated and that additional information would be valuable
tighter credit conditions for households and businesses are likely to weigh on economic activity, hiring, and inflation
Some participants commented that there continued to be downside risks to economic growth and upside risks to unemployment
the full effects of monetary tightening had likely yet to be observed
summary: The Committee unanimously held rates at 5.00-5.25% with hawkish guidance, as almost all participants projected additional rate increases in 2023 would be appropriate despite pausing to assess cumulative tightening effects and credit conditions.
